```go
func make(t Type, size ...IntegerType) Type
```
Allocates and initializes an object of type **slice**, **map**, or **chan** (only). Unlike `new`, make's return type is the same as the type of its argument, not a pointer to it.

**Slices:** `make([]T, len)` or `make([]T, len, cap)`
**Maps:** `make(map[K]V)` or `make(map[K]V, hint)`
**Channels:** `make(chan T)` or `make(chan T, bufSize)`

```go
func new(Type) *Type
```
Allocates zeroed storage for a new value of type Type and returns a pointer to it. Equivalent to `&T{}` for composite types.

```go
func append(slice []Type, elems ...Type) []Type
```
Appends elements to the end of a slice. If capacity is insufficient, a new underlying array is allocated and the data is copied.

```go
s = append(s, elem1, elem2)
s = append(s, anotherSlice...)
```

```go
func copy(dst, src []Type) int
```
Copies elements from src into dst, returning the number of elements copied (min of `len(src)` and `len(dst)`). Also supports copying from a string into `[]byte`.

```go
func delete(m map[Type]Type1, key Type)
```
Deletes the element with the specified key from the map. If the map is nil or there is no such element, `delete` is a no-op.

```go
func len(v Type) int
```
Returns the length of v: array/pointer to array, slice, map, string, or channel.

| Type | Result |
|------|--------|
| Array | number of elements |
| Slice | number of elements |
| Map | number of key-value pairs |
| String | number of bytes |
| Channel | number of queued elements |

```go
func cap(v Type) int
```
Returns the capacity of v: array/pointer to array, slice, or channel buffer.

For slices, `cap(s)` is the size of the underlying array, starting from `s[0]`.

```go
func close(c chan<- Type)
```
Closes a channel, indicating that no more values will be sent. Only the sender should close a channel, never the receiver. Sending on a closed channel causes a panic.

```go
func panic(v any)
```
Stops normal execution of the current goroutine. When a function F calls panic, normal execution of F stops immediately. Any functions that were deferred by F are run in LIFO order. Then F returns to its caller, which runs as if F called panic. The process continues up the stack until all functions in the current goroutine have returned, at which point the program crashes.

```go
func complex(r, i FloatType) ComplexType
```
Constructs a complex value from two floating-point values. If both arguments are `float32`, returns `complex64`; otherwise returns `complex128`.

```go
func real(c ComplexType) FloatType
```
Returns the real part of the complex number c.

```go
func imag(c ComplexType) FloatType
```
Returns the imaginary part of the complex number c.

```go
func clear[T ~[]Type | ~map[Type]Type1](t T)
```
Clears maps and slices. For maps, deletes all entries. For slices, sets all elements to the zero value up to len(s). Added in **Go 1.21**.

```go
func min[T cmp.Ordered](x T, y ...T) T
```
Returns the smallest value among the arguments. For floating-point, returns NaN if any argument is NaN. Added in **Go 1.21**.

```go
func max[T cmp.Ordered](x T, y ...T) T
```
Returns the largest value among the arguments. Added in **Go 1.21**.

```go
func print(args ...Type)
```
Formats its arguments in an implementation-specific way and writes the result to standard error. Useful for bootstrapping and debugging; not guaranteed to remain in the language. Use `fmt.Print` for production code.

```go
func println(args ...Type)
```
Like print but writes spaces between arguments and a newline at the end.

```go
type string
```
The set of all strings of 8-bit bytes, conventionally (but not necessarily) representing UTF-8-encoded text. A string may be empty but not nil. Values of type string are immutable.

```go
type bool
```
The set of boolean values: `true` and `false`.

```go
type byte = uint8
```
An alias for uint8. Used to distinguish byte values from 8-bit unsigned integer values.

```go
type rune = int32
```
An alias for int32. Represents a Unicode code point.

```go
type int
```
A signed integer type that is at least 32 bits in size. It is a distinct type, however, and not an alias for int32 or int64.

```go
type int8
```
The set of all signed 8-bit integers. Range: -128 through 127.

```go
type int16
```
The set of all signed 16-bit integers. Range: -32768 through 32767.

```go
type int32
```
The set of all signed 32-bit integers. Range: -2147483648 through 2147483647.

```go
type int64
```
The set of all signed 64-bit integers. Range: -9223372036854775808 through 9223372036854775807.

```go
type uint
```
An unsigned integer type that is at least 32 bits in size. A distinct type, not an alias.

```go
type uint8
```
The set of all unsigned 8-bit integers. Range: 0 through 255.

```go
type uint16
```
The set of all unsigned 16-bit integers. Range: 0 through 65535.

```go
type uint32
```
The set of all unsigned 32-bit integers. Range: 0 through 4294967295.

```go
type uint64
```
The set of all unsigned 64-bit integers. Range: 0 through 18446744073709551615.

```go
type uintptr
```
An integer type that is large enough to hold the bit pattern of any pointer.

```go
type float32
```
The set of all IEEE-754 32-bit floating-point numbers.

```go
type float64
```
The set of all IEEE-754 64-bit floating-point numbers.

```go
type complex64
```
The set of all complex numbers with float32 real and imaginary parts.

```go
type complex128
```
The set of all complex numbers with float64 real and imaginary parts.

```go
type any = interface{}
```
Alias for `interface{}`. Introduced in Go 1.18. Used as a type constraint meaning any type.
```go
func Print[T any](v T) { fmt.Println(v) }
```

```go
// nil
```
Predeclared identifier representing the zero value for pointers, channels, funcs, interfaces, maps, and slices.

```go
const true = 0 == 0
```
Boolean truth constant.

```go
const false = 0 != 0
```
Boolean falsehood constant.

```go
const iota = 0
```
Predeclared identifier representing the untyped integer ordinal of the current const specification in a (usually parenthesized) const declaration. It is zero-indexed.

```go
const (
    Sunday = iota  // 0
    Monday         // 1
    Tuesday        // 2
)
```

```go
var name Type = value
```
Declares one or more variables. If an initializer is present, the type can be omitted: `var x = 42`. Inside functions, the `:=` short form can be used instead.

```go
const name Type = value
```
Declares a constant value. Constants can be character, string, boolean, or numeric values. They cannot be declared using `:=`.

```go
type name underlying
```
Declares a new named type. Used for structs, interfaces, type aliases, and type definitions.

```go
type Person struct { Name string; Age int }
type Stringer interface { String() string }
type MyInt = int  // alias
```

```go
type Name interface { ... }
```
Defines a set of method signatures. A type implements an interface by implementing its methods — no explicit declaration needed (structural typing). In Go 1.18+, interfaces can also embed type constraints.

```go
map[KeyType]ValueType
```
A reference to a hash table. Maps must be initialized with `make` or a literal before use. The zero value is nil. Access, insertion, and deletion are average O(1).

```go
m := make(map[string]int)
m := map[string]int{"a": 1, "b": 2}
```

```go
chan Type / chan<- Type / <-chan Type
```
Provides a mechanism for goroutines to communicate by sending and receiving values. Channels can be **unbuffered** (synchronous) or **buffered** (asynchronous with capacity).

```go
ch := make(chan int)       // unbuffered
ch := make(chan int, 100)  // buffered
ch <- value                // send
v := <-ch                  // receive
```

```go
if condition { ... } else if condition { ... } else { ... }
```
Executes branches conditionally. Can include an init statement: `if err := f(); err != nil { ... }`

```go
if condition { ... } else { ... }
```
The else clause of an if statement. Must be on the same line as the closing brace of the if block.

```go
for init; condition; post { ... }
```
Go's only looping construct. Three forms:

```go
for i := 0; i < 10; i++ { }  // C-style
for condition { }              // while-style
for { }                        // infinite loop
for i, v := range slice { }   // range-based
```

```go
for index, value := range collection { ... }
```
Iterates over elements of arrays, slices, strings, maps, and channels.

| Type | 1st value | 2nd value |
|------|-----------|----------|
| array/slice | index | element |
| string | index | rune |
| map | key | value |
| channel | element | — |

In **Go 1.22+**, `range N` iterates integers from 0 to N-1.

```go
switch expr { case v1: ... default: ... }
```
Multi-way branch. Cases do **not** fall through by default (unlike C). Use `fallthrough` to force fall-through.

```go
switch os := runtime.GOOS; os {
case "darwin": fmt.Println("macOS")
case "linux": fmt.Println("Linux")
default: fmt.Println(os)
}
```

```go
case value:
```
A clause in a switch or select statement. Multiple values: `case 1, 2, 3:`. Type switch: `case int:`.

```go
default:
```
The default case in a switch or select statement, executed when no other case matches.

```go
select { case <-ch1: ... case ch2 <- v: ... default: ... }
```
Lets a goroutine wait on multiple communication operations. Blocks until one case can run, then executes it. If multiple are ready, one is chosen at random.

```go
select {
case msg := <-ch:
    fmt.Println(msg)
case <-time.After(5 * time.Second):
    fmt.Println("timeout")
}
```

```go
return value1, value2
```
Terminates execution of the function and optionally provides one or more result values. In functions with named results, a bare `return` returns the named values.

```go
break [label]
```
Terminates execution of the innermost `for`, `switch`, or `select` statement. With a label, breaks out of the labeled statement.

```go
continue [label]
```
Begins the next iteration of the innermost `for` loop. With a label, continues the labeled loop.

```go
goto Label
```
Transfers control to the labeled statement. The goto must not jump over variable declarations or into different blocks.

```go
fallthrough
```
Transfers control to the first statement of the next case clause in a switch. Can only be used as the final statement in a case.

```go
defer functionCall()
```
Pushes a function call onto a stack. The deferred call's arguments are evaluated immediately, but the function call is not executed until the surrounding function returns. Deferred calls are executed in **LIFO** order.

```go
defer file.Close()
defer mu.Unlock()
```

```go
package name
```
Every Go source file begins with a package clause. Executable programs must have `package main`. Package names are lowercase and follow the directory structure.

```go
import "path" / import ( ... )
```
Declares the packages that the source file depends on.

```go
import "fmt"
import (
    "net/http"
    "encoding/json"
)
```

```go
func fmt.Println(a ...any) (n int, err error)
```
Formats using default formats and writes to stdout. Spaces between operands, newline appended.

```go
func fmt.Printf(format string, a ...any) (n int, err error)
```
Formats according to a format specifier and writes to stdout.

Common verbs: `%v`=default, `%+v`=with fields, `%#v`=Go syntax, `%T`=type, `%d`=integer, `%s`=string, `%f`=float, `%t`=bool, `%p`=pointer, `%w`=wrap error.

```go
func fmt.Sprintf(format string, a ...any) string
```
Formats according to a format specifier and **returns the resulting string** (does not print).

```go
func fmt.Fprintf(w io.Writer, format string, a ...any) (n int, err error)
```
Formats according to a format specifier and writes to w.

```go
func fmt.Errorf(format string, a ...any) error
```
Formats according to a format specifier and returns it as an error. Use `%w` to **wrap** an error so it can be unwrapped with `errors.Is` and `errors.As`.

```go
return fmt.Errorf("connection failed: %w", err)
```

```go
func fmt.Sscanf(str string, format string, a ...any) (n int, err error)
```
Scans a string, reading values according to the format into successive arguments.

```go
func fmt.Fscanf(r io.Reader, format string, a ...any) (n int, err error)
```
Scans text read from r, storing successive space-separated values into successive arguments.

```go
func errors.New(text string) error
```
Returns an error that formats as the given text. Each call returns a distinct error value even for identical text.

```go
func errors.Is(err, target error) bool
```
Reports whether any error in err's **chain** matches target. An error's chain consists of the error itself followed by errors obtained by repeatedly calling `Unwrap`.

```go
if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) { ... }
```

```go
func errors.As(err error, target any) bool
```
Finds the first error in err's chain that matches target and sets target. Target must be a non-nil pointer to a type that implements error.

```go
var pathErr *os.PathError
if errors.As(err, &pathErr) { ... }
```

```go
func errors.Unwrap(err error) error
```
Returns the result of calling the Unwrap method on err, if err's type contains an Unwrap method returning error. Otherwise, returns nil.

```go
func errors.Join(errs ...error) error
```
Returns an error that wraps the given errors. Any nil error values are discarded. The Error method joins the error strings with newlines. Added in **Go 1.20**.

```go
func strings.Contains(s, substr string) bool
```
Reports whether substr is within s.

```go
func strings.HasPrefix(s, prefix string) bool
```
Tests whether the string s begins with prefix.

```go
func strings.HasSuffix(s, suffix string) bool
```
Tests whether the string s ends with suffix.

```go
func strings.Split(s, sep string) []string
```
Slices s into all substrings separated by sep and returns a slice. If sep is empty, splits after each UTF-8 sequence.

```go
func strings.Join(elems []string, sep string) string
```
Concatenates the elements of elems to create a single string. sep is placed between elements.

```go
func strings.Replace(s, old, new string, n int) string
```
Returns a copy with the first n non-overlapping instances of old replaced by new. If n < 0, replaces all.

```go
func strings.ReplaceAll(s, old, new string) string
```
Returns a copy with all non-overlapping instances of old replaced by new.

```go
func strings.TrimSpace(s string) string
```
Returns a slice with all leading and trailing white space removed, as defined by Unicode.

```go
func strings.Trim(s, cutset string) string
```
Returns a slice with all leading and trailing Unicode code points in cutset removed.

```go
func strings.ToUpper(s string) string
```
Returns s with all Unicode letters mapped to their upper case.

```go
func strings.ToLower(s string) string
```
Returns s with all Unicode letters mapped to their lower case.

```go
func strings.Index(s, substr string) int
```
Returns the index of the first instance of substr in s, or -1 if not present.

```go
func strings.Count(s, substr string) int
```
Counts the number of non-overlapping instances of substr in s. If substr is empty, returns 1 + number of Unicode code points.

```go
func strings.Repeat(s string, count int) string
```
Returns a new string consisting of count copies of s. Panics if count is negative or the result overflows.

```go
func strings.EqualFold(s, t string) bool
```
Reports whether s and t, interpreted as UTF-8, are equal under **case-folding** (case-insensitive comparison).

```go
func strings.Fields(s string) []string
```
Splits the string s around each instance of one or more consecutive white space characters, returning a slice.

```go
func strings.Map(mapping func(rune) rune, s string) string
```
Returns a copy with all characters modified according to the mapping function. If mapping returns -1, the character is dropped.

```go
func strings.NewReader(s string) *strings.Reader
```
Returns a new Reader reading from s. It implements io.Reader, io.ReaderAt, io.ByteReader, io.ByteScanner, io.RuneReader, io.RuneScanner, io.Seeker, and io.WriterTo.

```go
type strings.Builder struct{}
```
Used to efficiently build a string using `Write` methods. Minimizes memory copying. Use `Reset` to reuse.

```go
var b strings.Builder
b.WriteString("Hello")
b.WriteString(" World")
result := b.String()
```

```go
func strings.NewReplacer(oldnew ...string) *strings.Replacer
```
Returns a new Replacer from a list of old-new string pairs. Replacements are performed in the order they appear.

```go
func strings.CutPrefix(s, prefix string) (after string, found bool)
```
Returns s without the provided leading prefix string and reports whether it was found. Added in **Go 1.20**.

```go
func strings.CutSuffix(s, suffix string) (before string, found bool)
```
Returns s without the provided ending suffix string and reports whether it was found. Added in **Go 1.20**.

```go
func strings.Cut(s, sep string) (before, after string, found bool)
```
Slices s around the first instance of sep, returning text before and after sep. If sep is not found, returns s, "", false. Added in **Go 1.18**.

```go
func strconv.Itoa(i int) string
```
Equivalent to `FormatInt(int64(i), 10)`. Converts an integer to its decimal string representation.

```go
func strconv.Atoi(s string) (int, error)
```
Equivalent to `ParseInt(s, 10, 0)` converted to int. Parses a decimal string to int.

```go
func strconv.FormatInt(i int64, base int) string
```
Returns the string representation of i in the given base (2 to 36).

```go
func strconv.FormatFloat(f float64, fmt byte, prec, bitSize int) string
```
Converts a float to a string. fmt: 'f'=decimal, 'e'=exponent, 'g'=large exponent.

```go
func strconv.FormatBool(b bool) string
```
Returns `"true"` or `"false"` according to the value of b.

```go
func strconv.ParseInt(s string, base int, bitSize int) (int64, error)
```
Interprets a string s in the given base (0, 2 to 36) and bit size and returns int64.

```go
func strconv.ParseFloat(s string, bitSize int) (float64, error)
```
Converts string to float64. bitSize specifies precision: 32 for float32, 64 for float64.

```go
func strconv.ParseBool(str string) (bool, error)
```
Returns the boolean value represented by the string. Accepts 1, t, T, TRUE, true, True, 0, f, F, FALSE, false, False.

```go
func strconv.Quote(s string) string
```
Returns a double-quoted Go string literal representing s. Control characters and non-printable characters are escaped.

```go
func strconv.Unquote(s string) (string, error)
```
Interprets s as a single-quoted, double-quoted, or backquoted Go string literal.

```go
func strconv.AppendInt(dst []byte, i int64, base int) []byte
```
Appends the string form of the integer i to dst and returns the extended buffer.

```go
func os.Open(name string) (*os.File, error)
```
Opens the named file for **reading**. Equivalent to `OpenFile(name, O_RDONLY, 0)`. If successful, methods on the returned File can be used for reading.

```go
func os.Create(name string) (*os.File, error)
```
Creates or truncates the named file. Equivalent to `OpenFile(name, O_RDWR|O_CREATE|O_TRUNC, 0666)`. The file is open for reading and writing.

```go
func os.OpenFile(name string, flag int, perm os.FileMode) (*os.File, error)
```
The generalized open call. Most users will use Open or Create instead. Flags: `O_RDONLY`, `O_WRONLY`, `O_RDWR`, `O_APPEND`, `O_CREATE`, `O_EXCL`, `O_SYNC`, `O_TRUNC`.

```go
func os.ReadFile(name string) ([]byte, error)
```
Reads the named file and returns the contents. A successful call returns err == nil, not err == io.EOF.

```go
func os.WriteFile(name string, data []byte, perm os.FileMode) error
```
Writes data to the named file, creating it if necessary. If the file already exists, it is truncated before writing.

```go
func os.Remove(name string) error
```
Removes the named file or empty directory.

```go
func os.RemoveAll(path string) error
```
Removes path and any children it contains. Returns nil if the path does not exist.

```go
func os.Mkdir(name string, perm os.FileMode) error
```
Creates a new directory with the specified permission bits. Returns an error if the directory already exists.

```go
func os.MkdirAll(path string, perm os.FileMode) error
```
Creates a directory named path, along with any necessary parents, and returns nil, or else returns an error.

```go
func os.Rename(oldpath, newpath string) error
```
Renames (moves) oldpath to newpath. If newpath already exists and is not a directory, Rename replaces it.

```go
func os.Stat(name string) (os.FileInfo, error)
```
Returns a FileInfo describing the named file. If the file is a symlink, it returns info about the target.

```go
func os.Getenv(key string) string
```
Retrieves the value of the environment variable named by the key. Returns empty string if not set. Use `LookupEnv` to distinguish empty from unset.

```go
func os.Setenv(key, value string) error
```
Sets the value of the environment variable named by the key.

```go
func os.LookupEnv(key string) (string, bool)
```
Retrieves the value and reports whether the variable is present. Allows distinguishing empty from unset.

```go
func os.Exit(code int)
```
Causes the current program to exit with the given status code. Deferred functions are **not** run. Convention: code 0 = success, non-zero = error.

```go
func os.Getwd() (dir string, err error)
```
Returns the current working directory.

```go
func os.Chdir(dir string) error
```
Changes the current working directory.

```go
func os.TempDir() string
```
Returns the default directory to use for temporary files.

```go
func os.UserHomeDir() (string, error)
```
Returns the current user's home directory.

```go
var os.Args []string
```
Holds the command-line arguments, starting with the program name (`os.Args[0]`).

```go
var os.Stdin *os.File
```
Open files pointing to standard input. `os.Stdin`, `os.Stdout`, and `os.Stderr` are the standard Unix file descriptors.

```go
var os.Stdout *os.File
```
Standard output file descriptor.

```go
var os.Stderr *os.File
```
Standard error file descriptor.

```go
func (*os.File) Read(b []byte) (n int, err error)
```
Reads up to len(b) bytes. Returns the number of bytes read and any error. Returns 0, io.EOF at end of file.

```go
func (*os.File) Write(b []byte) (n int, err error)
```
Writes len(b) bytes. Returns the number of bytes written and any error. Returns an error when n != len(b).

```go
func (*os.File) Close() error
```
Closes the File, rendering it unusable for I/O. Returns an error, if any.

```go
func (*os.File) Name() string
```
Returns the name of the file as presented to Open.

```go
type io.ReadCloser interface {
    Reader
    Closer
}
```
Groups Reader and Closer interfaces. Common in HTTP response bodies.

```go
type io.ReadWriter interface {
    Reader
    Writer
}
```
Groups Reader and Writer interfaces.

```go
func io.ReadAll(r io.Reader) ([]byte, error)
```
Reads from r until EOF or error and returns the data. A successful call returns err == nil, not err == io.EOF.

```go
func io.Copy(dst io.Writer, src io.Reader) (written int64, err error)
```
Copies from src to dst until EOF or error. Returns the number of bytes copied and the first error encountered, if any.

```go
func io.CopyN(dst io.Writer, src io.Reader, n int64) (written int64, err error)
```
Copies n bytes from src to dst. Returns io.EOF if fewer than n bytes were available.

```go
func io.WriteString(w io.Writer, s string) (n int, err error)
```
Writes the contents of the string s to w. If w implements StringWriter, calls that directly.

```go
func io.NopCloser(r io.Reader) io.ReadCloser
```
Returns a ReadCloser with a no-op Close method wrapping the provided Reader r.

```go
func io.Pipe() (*io.PipeReader, *io.PipeWriter)
```
Creates a synchronous in-memory pipe. Reads block until data is written, writes block until data is read.

```go
func io.LimitReader(r io.Reader, n int64) io.Reader
```
Returns a Reader that reads from r but stops with EOF after n bytes.

```go
func io.TeeReader(r io.Reader, w io.Writer) io.Reader
```
Returns a Reader that writes to w what it reads from r. All reads from r are matched with writes to w.

```go
func io.MultiReader(readers ...io.Reader) io.Reader
```
Returns a Reader that is the logical concatenation of the provided readers.

```go
func io.MultiWriter(writers ...io.Writer) io.Writer
```
Creates a writer that duplicates its writes to all provided writers, similar to Unix tee(1).

```go
var io.EOF error
```
Returned by Read when no more input is available. Functions should return EOF to signal a graceful end of input.

```go
var io.ErrUnexpectedEOF error
```
Means that EOF was encountered in the middle of reading a fixed-size block or data structure.

```go
var io.Discard io.Writer
```
A Writer on which all Write calls succeed without doing anything. Equivalent to /dev/null.

```go
func bufio.NewReader(rd io.Reader) *bufio.Reader
```
Returns a new Reader with the default buffer size (4096 bytes).

```go
func bufio.NewWriter(w io.Writer) *bufio.Writer
```
Returns a new Writer with the default buffer size. Remember to call `Flush()` to write buffered data.

```go
func bufio.NewScanner(r io.Reader) *bufio.Scanner
```
Returns a new Scanner that reads from r. Split function defaults to `ScanLines`.

```go
scanner := bufio.NewScanner(os.Stdin)
for scanner.Scan() {
    fmt.Println(scanner.Text())
}
```

```go
func (*bufio.Scanner) Scan() bool
```
Advances the Scanner to the next token, which will be available through Text or Bytes. Returns false at EOF or error.

```go
func (*bufio.Scanner) Text() string
```
Returns the most recent token generated by Scan as a newly allocated string.

```go
func (*bufio.Scanner) Err() error
```
Returns the first non-EOF error encountered by the Scanner.

```go
type bytes.Buffer struct{}
```

Returns a new Encoder that writes to w.



```go

```

```go

mux := http.NewServeMux()
```

```go

```
An adapter to allow the use of ordinary functions as HTTP handlers. If f is a function with the appropriate signature, `HandlerFunc(f)` is a Handler that calls f.

```go
type http.ResponseWriter interface {
    Header() http.Header
    Write([]byte) (int, error)

}
```
Used by an HTTP handler to construct an HTTP response. Must call WriteHeader before writing the body if setting a non-200 status.

```go
type http.Request struct {
    Method string

    Header Header
    Body io.ReadCloser
    ...
}
```
Represents an HTTP request received by a server or to be sent by a client. Key fields: Method, URL, Header, Body, Form, Context().

```go
type http.Response struct {
    Status string
    StatusCode int
    Header Header
    Body io.ReadCloser
    ...
}
```
Represents the response from an HTTP request. **Always close Body** when done reading: `defer resp.Body.Close()`.

```go
type http.Client struct {

    ...
}
```

```go
}
```

```go
func context.Background() context.Context
```
Returns a non-nil, empty Context. It is never canceled, has no values, and has no deadline. Typically used in main, init, and tests, and as the top-level Context for incoming requests.

```go
func context.TODO() context.Context
```
Returns a non-nil, empty Context. Use when it's unclear which Context to use or when the surrounding function has not yet been extended to accept a Context parameter.

```go
func context.WithCancel(parent context.Context) (ctx context.Context, cancel context.CancelFunc)
```
Returns a copy of parent with a new Done channel. The returned cancel function must be called when operations are complete to release resources.

```go
ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
defer cancel()
```

```go
func context.WithTimeout(parent context.Context, timeout time.Duration) (context.Context, context.CancelFunc)
```
Returns `WithDeadline(parent, time.Now().Add(timeout))`. Cancels automatically after the timeout.

```go
ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 5*time.Second)
defer cancel()
```

```go
func context.WithDeadline(parent context.Context, d time.Time) (context.Context, context.CancelFunc)
```
Returns a copy of parent whose Done channel is closed when the deadline expires, when the returned cancel function is called, or when the parent's Done channel is closed.

```go
func context.WithValue(parent context.Context, key, val any) context.Context
```
Returns a copy of parent in which the value associated with key is val. Keys should be unexported types to avoid collisions.

```go
type ctxKey struct{}
ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, ctxKey{}, "value")
```

```go
func context.WithCancelCause(parent context.Context) (ctx context.Context, cancel context.CancelCauseFunc)
```
Like WithCancel but the cancel function takes an error cause. Retrieve with `context.Cause(ctx)`. Added in **Go 1.20**.

```go
func context.AfterFunc(ctx context.Context, f func()) (stop func() bool)
```
Arranges to call f in its own goroutine after ctx is done. Added in **Go 1.21**.

```go
type sync.Mutex struct{}
```
A mutual exclusion lock. The zero value is an unlocked mutex. Must not be copied after first use.

```go
var mu sync.Mutex
mu.Lock()
defer mu.Unlock()
// critical section
```

```go
type sync.RWMutex struct{}
```
A reader/writer mutual exclusion lock. Multiple readers or one writer at a time. The zero value is unlocked.

```go
var mu sync.RWMutex
mu.RLock()     // for readers
mu.RUnlock()
mu.Lock()      // for writers
mu.Unlock()
```

```go
func sync.OnceFunc(f func()) func()
```
Returns a function that invokes f only once. Added in **Go 1.21**.

```go
type sync.Map struct{}
```
A concurrent-safe map. Like a `map[any]any` but safe for concurrent use without additional locking. Optimized for two use cases: (1) each key is written once and read many times, (2) multiple goroutines read/write disjoint sets of keys.

Methods: `Load`, `Store`, `Delete`, `Range`, `LoadOrStore`, `LoadAndDelete`.

```go
type sync.Pool struct {
    New func() any
}
```
A set of temporary objects that may be individually saved and retrieved. Safe for concurrent use. Used to cache allocated but unused items for later reuse, relieving pressure on the garbage collector.

```go
pool := &sync.Pool{New: func() any { return new(bytes.Buffer) }}
buf := pool.Get().(*bytes.Buffer)
defer pool.Put(buf)
```

```go
func (*sync.Mutex) Lock()
```
Locks the mutex. If the lock is already in use, the calling goroutine blocks until the mutex is available.

```go
func (*sync.Mutex) Unlock()
```
Unlocks the mutex. Panics if not locked on entry.

```go
func (*sync.WaitGroup) Add(delta int)
```
Adds delta, which may be negative, to the WaitGroup counter. If the counter becomes zero, all goroutines blocked on Wait are released.

```go
func (*sync.WaitGroup) Done()
```
Decrements the WaitGroup counter by one. Equivalent to `Add(-1)`.

```go
func (*sync.WaitGroup) Wait()
```
Blocks until the WaitGroup counter is zero.

```go
type atomic.Int64 struct{}
```
An int64 that can be atomically loaded and stored. Methods: `Load`, `Store`, `Add`, `Swap`, `CompareAndSwap`. Added in **Go 1.19**.

```go
type atomic.Bool struct{}
```
A bool that can be atomically loaded and stored. Methods: `Load`, `Store`, `Swap`, `CompareAndSwap`. Added in **Go 1.19**.

```go
type atomic.Pointer[T any] struct{}
```
A pointer that can be atomically loaded and stored. Type-safe generic version. Added in **Go 1.19**.

```go
func time.Now() time.Time
```
Returns the current local time.

```go
func time.Since(t time.Time) time.Duration
```
Returns the time elapsed since t. Shorthand for `time.Now().Sub(t)`.

```go
func time.Until(t time.Time) time.Duration
```
Returns the duration until t. Shorthand for `t.Sub(time.Now())`.

```go
func time.Sleep(d time.Duration)
```
Pauses the current goroutine for at least the duration d. A negative or zero duration causes Sleep to return immediately.

```go
func time.After(d time.Duration) <-chan time.Time
```
Waits for the duration to elapse and then sends the current time on the returned channel. **Warning**: the underlying Timer is not recovered until it fires. Prefer `NewTimer` for efficiency.

```go
func time.Tick(d time.Duration) <-chan time.Time
```
A convenience wrapper for NewTicker. **Warning**: the underlying Ticker is never stopped and cannot be garbage collected. Use `NewTicker` and defer `Stop()` for long-running programs.

```go
func time.NewTicker(d time.Duration) *time.Ticker
```
Returns a new Ticker that sends the current time on its channel after each tick. Stop the ticker to release resources.

```go
ticker := time.NewTicker(1 * time.Second)
defer ticker.Stop()
for t := range ticker.C { ... }
```

```go
func time.NewTimer(d time.Duration) *time.Timer
```
Creates a new Timer that will send the current time on its channel after at least duration d.

```go
func time.Parse(layout, value string) (time.Time, error)
```
Parses a time string using the reference time layout. Go uses the reference time `Mon Jan 2 15:04:05 MST 2006` (1/2 3:4:5 6 -7). Common layouts:

| Layout | Example |
|--------|---------|
| `time.RFC3339` | `2006-01-02T15:04:05Z07:00` |
| `time.DateTime` | `2006-01-02 15:04:05` |
| `time.DateOnly` | `2006-01-02` |

```go
func time.ParseDuration(s string) (time.Duration, error)
```
Parses a string like `"300ms"`, `"1.5h"`, `"2h45m"`. Valid units: ns, us/µs, ms, s, m, h.

```go
type time.Time struct{}
```
Represents an instant in time with nanosecond precision. The zero value is January 1, year 1, 00:00:00 UTC.

Key methods: `Format`, `Add`, `Sub`, `Before`, `After`, `Equal`, `Unix`, `UnixMilli`, `UTC`, `Local`, `In`.

```go
type time.Duration int64
```
Represents the elapsed time between two instants as an int64 nanosecond count. Use constants: `time.Second`, `time.Minute`, `time.Hour`, `time.Millisecond`, `time.Microsecond`, `time.Nanosecond`.

```go
func (time.Time) Format(layout string) string
```
Returns a textual representation of the time. Uses Go's reference time `Mon Jan 2 15:04:05 MST 2006`.

```go
t.Format(time.RFC3339)           // "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
t.Format("2006-01-02")           // "2024-01-15"
t.Format("15:04")                // "10:30"
```

```go
func (time.Time) Add(d time.Duration) time.Time
```
Returns the time t+d.

```go
func (time.Time) Sub(u time.Time) time.Duration
```
Returns the duration t-u. To compute t-d for a duration d, use `t.Add(-d)`.

```go
func (time.Time) Before(u time.Time) bool
```
Reports whether the time instant t is before u.

```go
func (time.Time) After(u time.Time) bool
```
Reports whether the time instant t is after u.

```go
func (time.Time) Unix() int64
```
Returns t as a Unix time, the number of seconds elapsed since January 1, 1970 UTC.

```go
func (time.Time) UnixMilli() int64
```
Returns t as a Unix time, the number of milliseconds elapsed since January 1, 1970 UTC.


```go
type sql.DB struct{}
```
Database handle representing a pool of zero or more connections. Safe for concurrent use. Key methods:

| Method | Description |
|--------|------------|
| `Query/QueryContext` | SELECT (returns Rows) |
| `QueryRow/QueryRowContext` | Single-row SELECT |
| `Exec/ExecContext` | INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE |
| `Prepare` | Prepared statement |
| `Begin/BeginTx` | Start transaction |
| `Ping` | Verify connection |
| `Close` | Close pool |

```go
func (*sql.DB) Query(query string, args ...any) (*sql.Rows, error)
```
Executes a query that returns rows (typically SELECT). Always check Rows.Err() after iteration.

```go
rows, err := db.Query("SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE age > $1", 18)
defer rows.Close()
for rows.Next() {
    var id int; var name string
    rows.Scan(&id, &name)
}
```

```go
func (*sql.DB) QueryRow(query string, args ...any) *sql.Row
```
Executes a query expected to return at most one row. Always returns non-nil; errors deferred until `Row.Scan()`.

```go
var name string
err := db.QueryRow("SELECT name FROM users WHERE id=$1", 42).Scan(&name)
if err == sql.ErrNoRows { ... }
```

```go
func (*sql.DB) Exec(query string, args ...any) (sql.Result, error)
```
Executes a query that doesn't return rows (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE).

```go
result, err := db.Exec("INSERT INTO users (name, age) VALUES ($1, $2)", name, age)
id, _ := result.LastInsertId()
affected, _ := result.RowsAffected()
```

```go
type sql.Tx struct{}
```
An in-progress database transaction. Must end with `Commit()` or `Rollback()`.

```go
tx, err := db.Begin()
if err != nil { ... }
defer tx.Rollback()
// ... operations ...
tx.Commit()
```


```go
func bytes.Compare(a, b []byte) int
```


Returns a copy of the slice s with all non-overlapping instances of old replaced by new. If old is empty, it matches at the beginning of the slice and after each UTF-8 sequence, yielding up to k+1 replacements for a k-byte slice.



```go
return [values]
```
Terminates execution of the function and returns values to the caller.

```go
break [label]
```
Terminates execution of the innermost for, switch, or select statement.

```go
continue [label]
```
Begins the next iteration of the innermost for loop.

```go
fallthrough
```
Transfers control to the next case in a switch statement.

```go
goto Label
```
Transfers control to the labeled statement.

```go
if [init;] condition { ... } [else { ... }]
```
Conditional branch. Optional init statement separated by semicolon.

```go
if ... { ... } else { ... }
```
Alternative branch of an if statement.

```go
for [init; condition; post] { ... }
```
The only looping construct in Go. Can be used as while loop (for condition {}), infinite loop (for {}), or range loop.

```go
switch [init;] [tag] { case ...: ... default: ... }
```
Multi-way branch. Cases don't fall through by default.

```go
case value1, value2:
```
A branch in a switch or select statement.

```go
default:
```
The default branch in a switch or select statement.

```go
package name
```
Declares the package name. Must be the first declaration in every Go source file.

```go
import "path" / import ( ... )
```
Declares packages to import. Supports aliases, blank imports, and dot imports.

```go
func (*testing.T) Log(args ...any)
```
Formats its arguments using default formatting and records the text in the error log. For tests, the text will be printed only if the test fails or the -test.v flag is set.

```go
func (*testing.T) Logf(format string, args ...any)
```
Formats its arguments according to the format like Sprintf and records the text in the error log.

```go
func (*testing.T) Fail()
```
Marks the test as failed but continues execution.

```go
func (*testing.T) FailNow()
```
Marks the test as failed and stops its execution. Does not stop other tests.

```go
func (*testing.T) Failed() bool
```
Reports whether the test has been marked as failed.

```go
func (*testing.T) Skipf(format string, args ...any)
```
Equivalent to Logf followed by SkipNow.

```go
func (*testing.T) Name() string
```
Returns the name of the running test or benchmark.

```go
func (*testing.T) Skipped() bool
```
Reports whether the test was skipped.

```go
func (*testing.T) Setenv(key, value string)
```
Calls os.Setenv(key, value) and uses Cleanup to restore the environment variable to its original value after the test. Cannot be used in parallel tests.

```go
func (*testing.B) StartTimer()
```
Starts timing a test. Called automatically before a benchmark starts, but can be used to resume timing after StopTimer.

```go
func (*testing.B) StopTimer()
```
Stops timing a test. Can be used to pause the timer while performing complex initialization.
